schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

step.text: Pass special resolution at general meeting
step.name: Step 1

step.text: Clear all pending returns (ROC, IT, GST)
step.name: Step 2

step.text: Settle all liabilities and recover receivables
step.name: Step 3

step.text: File STK-2 with RoC for strike-off
step.name: Step 4

step.text: Receive confirmation of dissolution from RoC
step.name: Step 5

acceptedAnswer.text: Strike off under Section 248: 3-6 months. Requires nil assets/liabilities. Voluntary liquidation under IBC Section 59: 6-12 months.
All tax matters must be settled before closure. Income tax clearance certificate and GST cancellation required.

mainEntity.name: What are the modes of winding up a company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Two modes: (1) Voluntary winding up under Section 59 of IBC by special resolution (solvent companies) or ordinary resolution (insolvent). (2) Tribunal-ordered winding up under Section 271 of Companies Act. Strike-off under Section 248 is for defunct/dormant companies.

mainEntity.name: How long does company winding up take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Strike-off (Section 248): 3-6 months. Voluntary winding up (IBC Section 59): 6-12 months. Tribunal winding up: 12-36 months. Strike-off is fastest but requires no assets, no liabilities, and no pending litigation.

mainEntity.name: What are the conditions for strike-off under Section 248?

acceptedAnswer.text: Company must: (1) have no assets and liabilities or nil assets/liabilities on date of application, (2) all charges satisfied, (3) no pending proceedings against the company, (4) all regulatory dues cleared (GST, IT, ROC filings).

mainEntity.name: Is GST cancellation needed before winding up?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. File GST REG-16 for voluntary cancellation. Clear all pending returns and liabilities. Pay final tax. Final return (GSTR-10) to be filed within 3 months of cancellation order.

mainEntity.name: What happens to director DIN after company closure?

acceptedAnswer.text: DIN remains active. Director can use same DIN for future companies. If company was struck off due to non-compliance, directors face disqualification under Section 164(2) for 5 years from date of strike-off.
